summary: Cathy O'Neil celebrates the closing of Corinthian Colleges, Inc., a for-profit college that gamed the federal student loan system while providing worthless credentials. The company was caught lying about job placement rates (claiming 100% when reality was closer to 0%) and advertising nonexistent programs. Most disturbing was their deliberate targeting of vulnerable populations—low-income, isolated individuals with low self-esteem—using predatory marketing tactics. O'Neil expresses interest in understanding the data-driven methods behind this targeting and suspects data warehousing services played a role.
key_points: For-profit colleges like Corinthian survive almost entirely on federal student loans while delivering terrible services
CCI was caught fabricating job placement statistics and advertising programs that didn't exist
Internal company documents revealed deliberate targeting of vulnerable demographics described as 'isolated,' 'impatient,' with 'low self-esteem'
The predatory advertising included fake program listings designed to harvest potential students' contact information
O'Neil suspects data warehousing services enabled the sophisticated targeting of vulnerable populations
money_quotes: They survive almost entirely on federal student loans of their students, while delivering terrible services and worthless credentials.
In some cases, they said 100% when the truth was closer to 0%.

CCI describes its target demographic as 'isolated,' 'impatient,' individuals with 'low self-esteem,' who have 'few people in their lives who care about them' and who are 'stuck' and 'unable to see and plan well for future.'
I'm guessing it was substantially online, and I'm guessing they got help from data warehousing services.
key_insight: For-profit colleges weaponize data-driven marketing to systematically target society's most vulnerable people, extracting federal loan money while delivering nothing of value.
